subsection.heading: Operation, maintenance, and management contracts; penalty
subsection.chapeau: Any contract or portion thereof which is entered into after October 8, 1980, and which—

paragraph.content: provides for operation, maintenance, or management of a condominium or cooperative association in a conversion project, or of property serving the condominium or cooperative unit owners in such project;
is between such unit owners or such association and the developer or an affiliate of the developer;
was entered into while such association was controlled by the developer through special developer control or because the developer held a majority of the votes in such association; and
is for a period of more than three years, including any automatic renewal provisions which are exercisable at the sole option of the developer or an affiliate of the developer,

subsection.continuation: may be terminated without penalty by such unit owners or such association.

subsection.heading: Time of termination
subsection.chapeau: Any termination under this section may occur only during the two-year period beginning on the date on which—

paragraph.content: special developer control over the association is terminated; or
the developer owns 25 per centum or less of the units in the conversion project,

content.p: A termination under this section shall be by a vote of owners of not less than two-thirds of the units other than the units owned by the developer or an affiliate of the developer.
Following the unit owners’ vote, the termination shall be effective ninety days after hand delivering notice or mailing notice by prepaid United States mail to the parties to the contract.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: In subsec. (a), “October 8, 1980” was substituted for “the effective date of this title”. See Effective Date note set out under section 3601 of this title.
